projects.name: channel-manager
projects.progressLabel: Sprint A/B + bootstrap hardening complete with expanded API end-to-end flow coverage
currentGoal: Stabilize the active backend refactor, preserve the now-working local build/start path, and continue closing the highest-value runtime and frontend/backend contract gaps.
doneRecently: Confirmed the repo contains substantial breadth: 12 API controllers, 20+ application/service files, tenant-aware DbContexts, OTA adapters, payments, analytics, direct booking, and a React/Vite frontend with routed pages.
Confirmed the frontend builds successfully with `npm run build`.
Confirmed the current refactor heavily simplifies API wiring and OTA adapter implementations rather than adding net-new surface area.
Re-validated that `README.md` contains a warning not to trust “fully complete” claims at face value.
Added a first backend auth slice:
`POST /api/v1/auth/login`
`POST /api/v1/auth/refresh`
shared auth DTOs
Made JWT handling internally consistent by issuing JWT refresh tokens with a `token_type` claim instead of generating opaque refresh strings that the existing validation path could not understand.
Updated the web client to persist auth state and automatically send `X-Tenant-Slug` when the authenticated user includes one.
Updated the tenant middleware/auth pipeline ordering so authenticated requests can recover tenant slug from JWT claims when the header/subdomain is missing.
Added a session-level implementation log in `docs/WORK_LOG.md` for future agents.
Retargeted the backend solution from `net10.0` to `net8.0` so it builds with the available Homebrew SDK.
Added repo-local NuGet config (`NuGet.Config` and `src/api/NuGet.Config`) so restore succeeds even when the machine-level `nuget.org` source is disabled.
Verified the backend locally:
`dotnet restore src/api/ChannelManager.sln` succeeds
`dotnet build src/api/ChannelManager.sln --no-restore` succeeds
the API host starts and returns `200` on `/health/live` and `/health/ready`
Added a `NoOpEmailProvider` so messaging and tenant-admin no longer depend on a missing DI registration just to run.
Updated dashboard/reservations frontend pages to target the property-scoped backend endpoints that currently exist.
Added an authenticated `GET /api/v1/properties` endpoint (Inventory module) to support frontend property bootstrap.
Wired frontend property bootstrap in `AppLayout`:
fetches properties at authenticated app startup
hydrates property store and prefers `user.propertyId` when available
keeps URL `:propertyId` and selected property in sync
Updated the property store to preserve selected property across property-list refreshes.
Fixed channel sync update semantics in `ChannelSyncService`:
removed `AsNoTracking()` from push connection queries so `LastPushAt` writes persist
removed unsafe parallel fanout over a shared EF `DbContext` in push flows
Fixed maintenance frontend contract drift by switching to `GET /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/maintenance` and adapting backend maintenance DTO/enums to the existing work-order UI shape.
Added missing backend endpoints used by existing frontend pages:
`GET /api/v1/channels` for the channels page
`GET /api/v1/guests` and `GET /api/v1/guests/{id}` for guest list/detail pages
Added `GET /api/v1/reservations/{reservationId}/folio` compatibility endpoint so the existing folio page route can resolve against current backend data.
Moved `GET /api/v1/channels` into API host assembly after runtime discovery checks, and confirmed the endpoint now resolves through auth middleware (`401` unauthenticated instead of `404`).
Performed live unauthenticated route smoke under `dotnet run`:
`/health/live` -> `200`
`/api/v1/properties`, `/api/v1/channels`, `/api/v1/guests`, `/api/v1/reservations/{id}/folio` -> `401`
Started backend hardening implementation:
introduced SMTP-capable email provider and configurable no-op fallback behavior in core DI
tightened tenant-admin service validation paths (plan parsing, email normalization/validation, inviter validation, duplicate invitation prevention, invite-accept password/name checks)
switched tenant-admin invitation password hashing to shared core `PasswordHasher`
constrained tenant-admin audit-log retrieval to tenant-id markers instead of returning broad unscoped logs
Re-verified backend build/start after hardening changes.
Added engineering handoff artifact: `docs/SYSTEM_DESIGN_OVERVIEW.html` (architecture, tech stack, glossary, and key flow concepts).
Added optional startup bootstrap path (config-gated) to initialize missing public/tenant tables and seed local login context for validation runs.
Completed authenticated smoke validation with provided credentials and tenant slug:
`/api/v1/auth/login` -> `200`
`/api/v1/properties` -> `200` (empty list)
`/api/v1/channels` -> `200` (empty items)
`/api/v1/guests` -> `200` (empty items)
`/api/v1/reservations/{id}/folio` -> `404` for unknown reservation (expected)
`/api/v1/admin/tenants` -> `200`
Fixed reservation API runtime failures (`500`) caused by Npgsql v8 JSON handling by enabling dynamic JSON through a shared `NpgsqlDataSource` wired into all registered DbContexts.
Expanded bootstrap deterministic sample IDs/data beyond guest/reservation/folio:
seeded chain/property/room type/room/channel connection/sync log IDs
preserved and reused existing guest/reservation/folio/folio-charge deterministic IDs
Re-validated authenticated seeded-data smoke responses:
`/api/v1/properties` -> `200` with seeded property
`/api/v1/channels` -> `200` with seeded channel connection
`/api/v1/guests` -> `200` with seeded guest
`/api/v1/reservations/{id}` -> `200` (fixed from prior `500`)
`/api/v1/reservations?propertyId={id}` -> `200` (fixed from prior `500`)
`/api/v1/reservations/{id}/folio` -> `200`
Added deterministic handoff docs at `docs/TEST_IDS.md` for cross-agent validation consistency.
Hardened bootstrap table initialization behavior:
added mapped-table existence pre-check to skip unnecessary `CreateTablesAsync` calls for fully initialized contexts
kept tolerant catch/continue for existing table/schema states to avoid bootstrap crash in overlapping-module table-name scenarios (for example `Requests`)
Added automated backend smoke test suite:
new project `src/api/ChannelManager.SmokeTests`
validates auth + deterministic seeded endpoints against a running API via env-configured base URL and credentials
added to solution and verified passing locally
Completed a read-only competitor observation pass against Asiatech admin:
captured action trace + screenshots under `docs/competitive/asiatech/20260403-184042/`
generated `docs/competitive/ASIATECH_COMPARISON_PLAN.md` with parity/missing-feature roadmap
Implemented Sprint A feature slice from competitor-gap plan:
Today Check-In/Out board backend + frontend flow
Booking Report backend + frontend flow
Credit Report backend + frontend flow
Added new backend endpoints:
`GET /api/v1/front-office/today-board/{propertyId}?date=YYYY-MM-DD`
`GET /api/v1/reservations/reports/booking?propertyId=...&from=...&to=...&status=...`
`GET /api/v1/reservations/reports/credit?propertyId=...&from=...&to=...`
Upgraded web surfaces:
`FrontOffice` now uses live operations-board data with arrivals/departures/in-house tabs and check-in/check-out actions
`Reservations` now includes tabbed Reservation List + Booking Report + Credit Report with filter controls and summary cards
Re-validated current runtime:
backend build passes
frontend build passes
authenticated endpoint smoke for all three new Sprint A endpoints returns `200`
Completed Sprint A follow-through:
added direct sidebar links for report deep-links:
`/reservations?tab=bookingReport`
`/reservations?tab=creditReport`
expanded smoke tests to cover Sprint A report endpoints and check-in/check-out mutation flow
verified smoke suite result: `Passed: 3, Failed: 0`
Started Sprint B implementation:
added list rate plans API (`GET /api/v1/properties/{propertyId}/rates/rate-plans`)
upgraded rate manager UI from placeholder to operational tabs:
linked channel-rate comparison
one-click bulk day update
quick seasonal multi-day uplift
added deterministic seeded rate plan/rate for local parity testing
Completed Sprint B lifecycle scope:
added persisted linked-rate rules lifecycle (`GET/POST/DELETE /rates/linked-rules`)
added persisted season lifecycle (`GET/POST/DELETE /rates/seasons`)
added persisted OTA discount lifecycle (`GET/POST/DELETE /rates/ota-discounts`)
upgraded rates calculation path to apply linked-rule offsets, seasonal adjustments, and OTA discounts in one flow
upgraded frontend `RateManager` with lifecycle tabs for season rules, OTA discounts, and linked rules
added second seeded deterministic rate plan/rate to support linked-rule testing
added bootstrap fallback to ensure new lifecycle tables exist idempotently even on partially initialized tenant schemas
fixed jsonb channel-filter runtime failure in rate calculation by moving season-channel matching to in-memory filtering
Expanded smoke coverage and re-validated end-to-end:
added `SprintB_RatesLifecycleEndpoints_WorkAndAffectRateCalculation`
smoke result: `Passed: 4, Failed: 0`
Completed bootstrap hardening cleanup:
removed EF raw SQL bootstrap inserts in favor of EF-native seeding for reservations/folio entities
replaced lifecycle table ensure raw SQL calls with safe ADO execution using validated schema identifiers
removed EF1002 warning sources from API bootstrap code path
Expanded automated verification depth:
added cross-module API E2E flow suite at `src/api/ChannelManager.SmokeTests/EndToEndFlowTests.cs`
full smoke+E2E suite now reports `Passed: 5, Failed: 0`
Fixed runtime issues surfaced by new E2E:
maintenance comment write path no longer throws concurrency exceptions
maintenance comment serialization no longer cycles on `Request` navigation property
Added browser-level Playwright E2E coverage in `src/web`:
added Playwright config and npm scripts (`test:e2e`, `test:e2e:headed`)
added browser flow spec covering login, dashboard/reservations tabs, front-office board, and rates lifecycle create/delete paths
added `src/web/e2e/README.md` runbook and ignored Playwright output artifacts in `.gitignore`
Verified browser E2E end-to-end against live local runtime:
restarted API on `http://127.0.0.1:5000` with bootstrap seed flags enabled
ran Playwright against web app on `http://127.0.0.1:4180`
result: `3 passed`
Wired browser E2E into GitHub Actions CI:
updated `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to use `.NET 8`
added dedicated `browser-e2e` job with:
Postgres service
API restore/build
web `npm ci`
Playwright Chromium install (`--with-deps`)
bootstrapped API startup with deterministic seeded tenant/user for login
web dev server startup + health waits
Playwright run with CI credentials/env
artifact uploads for Playwright report/test-results and service logs
updated docker job dependencies to require `browser-e2e` success
Fixed frontend CI script mismatch:
added `type-check` script (`tsc -b`) to `src/web/package.json` so existing frontend CI step is valid
inProgress: The worktree is still actively dirty across:
API host wiring (`Program.cs`)
OTA adapters (`AgodaAdapter`, `AirbnbAdapter`, etc.)
payments (`StripePaymentGateway`)
tenant admin services/persistence
many project files retargeted to `net8.0`
Current diff stats are still dominated by deletions, indicating an unfinished simplification/refactor rather than a settled implementation pass.
The repo now has a working local backend build/start path and property bootstrap wiring, but runtime correctness beyond targeted smoke/build checks still needs module-by-module validation.
nextUp: Continue tenant-admin endpoint validation depth and tighten placeholder/degraded paths that still return heuristic values.
Reconcile remaining frontend/backend contract mismatches on pages that still assume richer DTO shapes than current API responses.
Decide whether to harden the placeholder `NoOpEmailProvider` path or replace it with a real configured provider implementation.
Decide whether tenant-admin should be fully supported now or temporarily pared back, then align its service layer to the current core entities instead of returning placeholder values.
Validate first green GitHub Actions run and tune any CI-only flake/timeouts if surfaced.
blockers: Automated tests now exist at two levels:
backend API smoke/E2E (`src/api/ChannelManager.SmokeTests`)
browser E2E (`src/web/e2e`)
Browser E2E in CI depends on startup timing for API/web services; if CI runners are slow, timeout tuning may be required.
The backend currently depends on the repo-local NuGet config to recover from a machine-level disabled `nuget.org` source; future environments may still need the same fix.
The new properties bootstrap endpoint currently maps property contact fields (`phone`, `email`, `website`) to empty placeholders because those fields are not present on the current property entity.
`NoOpEmailProvider` keeps runtime DI healthy but does not actually deliver mail, so invitations/messages are still operationally stubbed.

Tenant-admin currently contains placeholder/degraded behavior in several paths (for example zero user counts and weakened audit log filtering), which suggests refactor drift rather than a finished admin surface.
The docs still overstate production readiness relative to the current codebase state.
The auth slice currently uses stateless JWT refresh tokens; revocation/rotation persistence is not yet implemented.
Tenant-aware login/runtime behavior is improved, but tenant context still depends on a resolvable tenant slug. Local multi-tenant developer ergonomics may need a more explicit tenant-selection path.
`.env` exists locally, so environment-sensitive work should avoid exposing secrets in commits or notes.
repoNotes: Backend appears architecturally broad but not yet proven end-to-end:
multi-tenant context and schema-aware DbContexts exist
core PMS modules (inventory, reservations, front office, folio, maintenance, guest experience, messaging, analytics, direct booking) all have service/controller code
several frontend pages are still static or only lightly wired, so web breadth exceeds current runtime integration depth
Highest-confidence integration gaps identified in this session:
several UI screens still assume richer domain data (for example full property contact data) than the current entities expose
tenant-admin still lacks true tenant-to-user relational modeling in core entities, so some admin views remain heuristic/limited even after validation hardening

narrative.currentGoal: Stabilize the active backend refactor, preserve the now-working local build/start path, and continue closing the highest-value runtime and frontend/backend contract gaps.
narrative.inProgress: The worktree is still actively dirty across:

projects.name: pdf-esigner
projects.progressLabel: SDK 1.2.7 viewer stabilization, public SEO pages, docs crawl improvements, and the outreach-app split are merged; remaining work is external distribution, response tracking, and operational follow-through
currentGoal: Finish the last release-facing polish around `Aoexl Sign`: keep the live product stable, maintain a consumer-safe docs surface, preserve a clear internal handoff trail, and close the remaining launch/outreach gaps without regressions.
doneRecently: Finished another public SEO pass and deployed it live:
added standard favicon/search assets plus `site.webmanifest`
removed `/login` from the sitemap and added explicit public route coverage for `/docs` and `/tools`
added more keyword landing pages for comparison/workflow intent (`/hellosign-api-alternative`, `/online-pdf-signer`)
strengthened blog/docs internal linking and structured data for crawlability
fixed docs-page SEO dispatch so docs canonicals/titles no longer rely on a broken fallback variable

Prepared an external SEO/distribution launch kit with Product Hunt copy, listing blurbs, Search Console follow-through URLs, and community-post drafts in `docs/seo-external/DISTRIBUTION_LAUNCH_KIT.md`.
Added a condensed execution guide for launch day in `docs/seo-external/LAUNCH_DAY_CHECKLIST.md` so Product Hunt + Search Console + first-wave distribution can happen in one focused session.
Merged the SDK/viewer stabilization and outreach split into `master`:
`@aoexl/sign` is prepared as `1.2.7`
SDK consumers can use `base` for self-hosted assets
first-load zoom can use `config.initialViewMode: "fit-width"`
flattened-document messaging is configurable
small PDFs eager-render to reduce short-document scroll stutter
engine asset loading starts in parallel with license validation
stale signing fields are cleared when opening/uploading another PDF
SDK tests pass with `57 passed`
Removed outreach from the customer-facing app route/sidebar and moved it into the standalone private `outreach-app` workspace.
Added internal change documentation at `docs-site/internal/operations/change-summary-2026-04-20.md`.
Added `Images to PDF` to the public PDF tools surface, including single/multi-image conversion, reorder, page size, fit mode, margins, and output naming.
Reworked the public landing/navigation so PDF tools are the lead wedge and the marketing nav exposes the real current feature surface more clearly.
Replaced stale third-party-branded public demo screenshots and sample PDFs with neutral Aoexl demo collateral.
Cleaned the public demo viewer chrome so the embedded workspace no longer collides with the marketing nav while scrolling.
Added a compact mobile marketing menu so the public header exposes product, tools, developer, resource, pricing, sign-in, and contact destinations on small screens.
Reworked `/developers` into a more documentation-like in-app hub that links quickstarts, guides, API docs, webhooks, and dashboard operations while preserving existing SDK/demo/webhook functionality.
Added stable `/developers#...` deep links for the Developer Hub docs/articles and major page sections.
Added multi-tenant MCP distribution groundwork:
in-app MCP API key generation/revocation in Settings
per-user API-key validation flow for the hosted MCP Worker
docs-site guide for Claude Desktop / Cursor MCP setup
publishable `aoexl-mcp` npm wrapper plus Smithery / MCP Registry metadata
Added app/docs SEO basics:
route-aware metadata for public app routes
`noindex,nofollow` for signer/login/authenticated routes
`robots.txt` and `sitemap.xml` for both `aoexl.com` and `docs.aoexl.com`
Stabilized the hosted signer flow across desktop/mobile, including compact signer chrome, floating progress UI, drag/touch improvements, next-field navigation, and completion handling.
Fixed live signing request creation, reopening/cancel semantics, route refresh persistence, sequential routing behavior, and source-PDF sync behavior needed for live hosted signing.
Completed a broad SDK/outreach pass: package build fixes, engine upload flow, consumer-app integration work, and public docs alignment with the current API/SDK surface.
Cleaned the public docs surface so internal runbooks/strategy are no longer consumer-visible, then added public-safe screenshots and code snippets across guides/API/blog pages.
Automated first-batch outreach prep and sending:
`npm run outreach:prep` generates the send sheet and queue.
`npm run outreach:send` sends source-backed rows through Resend and writes a ledger/report.
Copperlane and Veritus were sent successfully.
Mayflower, EigenPal, and Brickwise were skipped/held automatically instead of guessing unverified emails.
Re-ran the main build/test/smoke suite and recorded the latest exhaustive status in `docs-site/internal/operations/testing-status-2026-04-11-exhaustive.md`.
Fixed live signing-request request creation and invite delivery hardening:
`create-signing-request` no longer fails at the Supabase gateway with `401 Invalid JWT`
production SendGrid secrets are configured
invite delivery now succeeds again from the production edge flow
provider fallback logic exists so missing SendGrid config does not silently kill invites in the future
inProgress: The repo is now being maintained from `master` after a long integration/fix phase; launch-readiness work is mostly incremental rather than foundational.
Outreach can continue internally through `outreach-app`, but it should not be deployed publicly unless protected by Cloudflare Access or equivalent internal auth.
Public docs are now consumer-safe, but they still need periodic judgment calls so internal/ops material does not drift back into the public surface.
Final ops/outreach follow-through is still ongoing: response tracking, reply/objection handling, email/domain sanity checks, and any last-mile regressions found during manual usage.
Session-expiry handling in the sender UI still needs a final UI-layer decision:
the shared signing-request service now returns a typed `session_expired` error after retry
redirect/login recovery should be handled by pages/auth boundaries, not inside the shared service itself
nextUp: If deploying the public app, build/deploy only `pdf-esigner`; do not deploy `outreach-app` to the public site.
Decide whether generated outreach reports should remain committed as private audit artifacts or be moved to ignored storage.
Track replies and objections from the first automated outreach sends in `outreach/FIRST_BATCH_TRACKER.md`.
Add source-backed emails for Mayflower, EigenPal, and Brickwise if found; the sender will pick them up on a future run.
Keep the outreach claim discipline: lead with PDF tools plus embedded/hosted signing, and do not claim provider-backed identity verification as live.
Keep the launch/outreach checklist current as remaining manual/operator items close.
Continue tightening polish issues found during real usage, especially around send/email edge cases, signer UX, and consumer integrations.
Add a deliberate UI response for `session_expired` in sender-facing pages/panels instead of relying on service-layer redirects.
Preserve the public/internal docs split and keep handoff notes current whenever release-facing behavior changes.
Finish any remaining collateral/workflow updates needed for outbound outreach and broader launch confidence.
Finish MCP launch operations outside the repo:
deploy the hosted Worker with production secrets
mirror the connector into a small public repo if registry metadata should point somewhere public
publish the MCP listing surfaces and add the connect badge on the marketing site
Use Search Console to request indexing for `/`, `/pricing`, `/developers`, `/docs`, `/blog`, and the keyword pages now that the updated sitemap/routes are live
blockers: The repo lives inside a shared git root, so unrelated sibling changes can still confuse task ownership and status interpretation.
`.env` exists locally, so setup-sensitive work must avoid exposing secrets or license details.
Some final launch items remain operational rather than code-only: provider account readiness, production email health, support/contact plumbing, and human validation steps.
Public docs now intentionally exclude internal ops/strategy; future edits must maintain that separation.

narrative.currentGoal: Finish the last release-facing polish around `Aoexl Sign`: keep the live product stable, maintain a consumer-safe docs surface, preserve a clear internal handoff trail, and close the remaining launch/outreach gaps without regressions.
narrative.inProgress: The repo is now being maintained from `master` after a long integration/fix phase; launch-readiness work is mostly incremental rather than foundational.

projects.name: pawPetrol
projects.progressLabel: both pipelines are documented; detective_dog_ai now has hardened proof runs across all supported characters plus manifests and quality gating
currentGoal: Make `detective_dog_ai` reproducible enough to become the explicit primary pipeline, while keeping `detective-biscuit-factory` as a documented reference until that decision is formalized.
doneRecently: Confirmed the repo is really two separate nested Python projects with different maturity levels:
`detective-biscuit-factory`: a linear prototype pipeline with story, planning, images, narration, subtitles, audio mix, and export stages
`detective_dog_ai`: a richer pipeline with character rules, validation and retry loops, scene graphing, asset generation/caching, narration, timing, overlays, subtitles, and export
Verified `detective-biscuit-factory` has one complete checked-in artifact set, but the core generators still write mock story, image, audio, and video data.
Verified `detective_dog_ai` has many saved episode JSON files and exported reels, which is the strongest evidence that this is the project that has actually been iterated locally.
Added root and subproject `README.md` files so future agents have an execution-oriented handoff path instead of relying only on source inspection.
Added `detective_dog_ai/.env.example` to document the current environment surface.
Fixed the most obvious `detective_dog_ai` handoff traps:
character metadata now follows the selected character in the scene graph path
character profile loading is more tolerant of mixed YAML schemas
the pipeline no longer mixes background music twice at final export
Ran and documented a full `detective_dog_ai` validation pass for `detective_cat`, producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_024.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_014.mp4`
Updated subtitle handling in `detective_dog_ai` so missing FFmpeg subtitle-filter support is detected once and logged clearly.
Ran a real-key `detective_dog_ai` pass (OpenAI story + TTS active), producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_030.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_015.mp4`
Added retention fallbacks for validator stability when the model misses strict question/catchphrase requirements.
Completed a subtitle-burn proof run using subtitle-capable FFmpeg, producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_032.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_016.mp4`
burned-subtitle scene clips in `detective_dog_ai/data/clips/subbed_scene_*.mp4`
Added configurable narration quality modes to `detective_dog_ai` and validated `natural` mode with:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_033.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_017.mp4`
Added completion-time usage reporting in the pipeline; direct credit-balance lookup remains limited by OpenAI secret-key billing API restrictions.
Added quality-gated story retries to `detective_dog_ai`, so weak-but-valid stories can be retried before the expensive media stages.
Added run manifests and explicit asset-fallback telemetry to `detective_dog_ai`, making it clearer when a successful run still depended on mock visual generation.
Completed a real-key + subtitle-burn + `natural` narration proof run for `detective_bunny`, producing:
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_036.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_019.mp4`
`detective_dog_ai/data/manifests/20260403T081630Z_episode_036_success.json`
Fixed `detective_dog_ai` batch-export cleanup so source clips are explicitly released between episodes.
Completed hardened proof runs for the remaining supported `detective_dog_ai` characters:
`detective_biscuit`
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_039.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_020.mp4`
`detective_dog_ai/data/manifests/20260403T082036Z_episode_039_success.json`
`detective_cat`
`detective_dog_ai/data/episodes/episode_040.json`
`detective_dog_ai/data/reels/episode_021.mp4`
`detective_dog_ai/data/manifests/20260403T082454Z_episode_040_success.json`
inProgress: The repo direction is still drifting from the simpler Biscuit prototype toward `detective_dog_ai`, but the handoff docs do not yet state that as a final canonical decision.
`detective_dog_ai` has substantial uncommitted work around characters, narration, validation, subtitles, scheduling, Docker, and scripts.
`detective_dog_ai` now has hardened proof coverage for all supported characters, but its visual generation remains partly mock-backed.
nextUp: Add a real music asset and prove end-to-end background music mixing in `detective_dog_ai`.
Finish the remaining `detective_dog_ai` cleanup around unused/stale paths and validate all supported characters with the same hardened runbook.
Decide whether `detective-biscuit-factory` remains maintained or should be explicitly labeled as superseded/reference-only.
Replace or explicitly de-scope the mock visual-generation path in `detective_dog_ai` if production imagery is required.
blockers: The repo contains overlapping pipelines, which increases drift and makes it unclear which code path is authoritative.
`detective_dog_ai` still contains mock fallbacks for visual generation stages; manifests now surface that more clearly, but the dependency is still a product risk.
`detective-biscuit-factory` outputs are placeholder artifacts rather than proof of real media generation.
Local model, asset, FFmpeg, and API-key dependencies are implied by the code but still are not documented at the root.
Because the root folder is not its own git repo, status can be missed unless agents also inspect the nested repos directly.

narrative.currentGoal: Make `detective_dog_ai` reproducible enough to become the explicit primary pipeline, while keeping `detective-biscuit-factory` as a documented reference until that decision is formalized.
narrative.inProgress: The repo direction is still drifting from the simpler Biscuit prototype toward `detective_dog_ai`, but the handoff docs do not yet state that as a final canonical decision.

projects.name: verifyAI
projects.progressLabel: MVP frontend and backend are scaffolded, but product docs, testing, and operational polish are still thin
currentGoal: Mature the current proof of concept into a documented, testable product for evaluating challenge or verification-system resilience.
doneRecently: Built a React + Vite frontend with a landing page, input flow, result cards, and recommendation display.
Built a Node/Express backend with:
health endpoint
capture flow
AI-analysis call
score calculation
Wired the frontend to call the backend and display the returned analysis, risk level, and captured image.
Added Dockerfiles plus a root `docker-compose.yml`.
Added a root README documenting the product shape, local run path, and current MVP limitations.
inProgress: The app is beyond a blank scaffold, but several pieces still read like a prototype:
frontend README is still the default Vite template
backend has no real test script
the operational setup is not documented from the root
nextUp: Replace the frontend template README with product-specific docs and add a root README.
Add one reproducible local run path that covers frontend, backend, and any model dependencies.
Add tests around score calculation and API-level validation, then record known limitations clearly.
blockers: The root project has no README, which makes handoff quality poor.
The git history is tied to the shared `/Users/aryansingh/repos` root rather than a dedicated repo.
The backend currently exposes only a narrow test flow and lacks confidence-building test coverage.

narrative.currentGoal: Mature the current proof of concept into a documented, testable product for evaluating challenge or verification-system resilience.
narrative.inProgress: The app is beyond a blank scaffold, but several pieces still read like a prototype:
